text: The surgeon raises a flap and begins medial dissection during a robotic right inguinal hernia repair, revealing an unusual veil crossing the internal ring with the hernia located on the medial aspect, lateral to the epigastric vessels.

The surgeon identifies the pubic bone and begins lateral dissection, carefully hugging the peritoneum to stay within the proper plane. The cord structures are swept off the peritoneum, revealing a veil-like structure anteriorly above the scissors.

The surgeon demonstrates the anatomical positioning of the spermatic cord entering the canal lateral to a veil, while the hernia is situated medial to this veil. The hernia is reduced, revealing the space lateral to the epigastric vessels, with the spermatic cord structures positioned laterally and the hernia medially within the indirect space.

The surgeon continues to parietalize the spermatic cord, checking for a cord lipoma. Nodal tissue that had entered the ring is reduced. A 16 by 12 anatomic progrip mesh is placed, and the flap is closed.

The surgeon suspends a large hernia sac, demonstrating the technique used in this case. After undocking, the gas is evacuated laparoscopically using an NG catheter under laparoscopic vision, ensuring the gas is removed from the flap and confirming the mesh is in good position.
